title: pelazoneuron patens
intro: Pelazoneuron patens, also known as Pelargonium sidoides, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Geraniaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to combat respiratory infections, such as the common cold and flu, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pelazoneuron patens include reducing the severity and duration of upper respiratory tract infections, as well as alleviating symptoms such as congestion and coughing. Pelazoneuron patens is commonly prepared as a herbal tincture, tea, or syrup, and is often used as a natural remedy to support immune function and overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Indian journal of experimental biology", pelazoneuron patens (also known as C. peltata) benefits include lowering blood glucose and lipid levels, as well as increasing insulin and glycogen levels in type 2 diabetic rats.
